item.ptxt: When the automatic transaxle is replaced, the transaxle's compensation code must be input into the TCM (See page ).
When the TCM is replaced, the existing transaxle compensation codes must be input into the new TCM (See page ).
If the following parts have been replaced, initialize the TCM and perform a road test to allow the TCM to learn (See page ).
Valve body assembly
Shift solenoid SL3
Shift solenoid SL4
If the following parts have been replaced, perform a road test to allow the TCM to learn (See page ).
Shift solenoid SL1
Shift solenoid SL2
If the TCM or transaxle has been replaced, register the transmission compensation code in the TCM (See page ).
Perform RESET MEMORY (AT initialization) when replacing the automatic transmission assembly, engine assembly or ECM (See page ).

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL
PLACE FRONT WHEELS FACING STRAIGHT AHEAD
REMOVE FRONT FENDER APRON SEAL RH

atten3.ptxt: Do not remove the radiator cap sub-assembly while the engine and radiator are still hot. Pressurized, hot engine coolant and steam may be released and cause serious burns.

s2.ptxt: Remove the radiator cap sub-assembly from the radiator assembly.
Loosen the radiator drain cock plug and 2 cylinder block drain cock plugs, then drain the coolant.

atten4.ptxt: Collect the coolant in a container and dispose of it according to the regulations in your area.

s2.ptxt: Remove the refill plug and gasket.
Using a 6 mm socket hexagon wrench, remove the overflow plug and gasket.
Using a 6 mm socket hexagon wrench, remove the No. 1 transmission oil filler tube.
Drain the automatic transaxle fluid.
Using a 6 mm socket hexagon wrench, install the No. 1 transmission oil filler tube.
Using a 6 mm socket hexagon wrench, install the overflow plug with a new gasket.
Install the refill plug with a new gasket.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE COWL TOP PANEL OUTER SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the 4 bolts, 4 nuts and cowl top panel outer sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE COOL AIR INTAKE DUCT SEAL

s2.ptxt: Remove the 7 clips and intake duct seal.
Hold the front of the V-bank cover and raise it to disengage the 2 clips on the front of the cover. Continue to raise the cover to disengage the clip on the rear of the cover and remove the cover.

atten3.ptxt: Attempting to disengage both front and rear clips at the same time may cause the cover to break.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, release the belt tension by turning the belt tensioner counterclockwise, and remove the V-ribbed belt from the belt tensioner.
While turning the belt tensioner counterclockwise, align with its holes, and then insert the 5 mm bi-hexagon wrench into the holes to fix the V-ribbed belt tensioner.
Remove the 2 bolts, clamp and air cleaner inlet.
Disconnect the 3 vacuum hoses.
Disconnect the mass air flow meter connector (*1).
Disconnect the No. 2 ventilation hose (*2).
Disconnect the hose band (*3).
Disconnect the 3 bands, and remove the air cleaner cap sub-assembly.
Disconnect the vacuum hose and hose clamp.
Remove the 3 bolts and air cleaner case.
Remove the bolt and No. 1 air cleaner inlet.
Loosen the bolt and nut, and remove the battery clamp.
Remove the battery and battery tray.
Remove the clip, bolt and intake air resonator.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 2 ENGINE MOUNTING STAY RH

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt, 2 nuts, and No. 2 mounting stay RH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE ENGINE MOVING CONTROL ROD SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the 4 bolts and engine moving control rod.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT NO. 1 FUEL VAPOR FEED HOSE

s2.ptxt: Remove the clamp and disconnect the No. 1 fuel vapor feed hose.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT CHECK VALVE TO BRAKE BOOSTER HOSE

s2.ptxt: Remove the clamp and disconnect the check valve to brake booster hose.
Remove the clamp and disconnect the radiator hose inlet.
Remove the clamp and disconnect the radiator hose outlet.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT NO. 1 OIL COOLER INLET HOSE

s2.ptxt: Remove the clamp and disconnect the oil cooler inlet hose.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT NO. 1 OIL COOLER OUTLET HOSE

s2.ptxt: Remove the clamp and disconnect the oil cooler outlet hose.
Disconnect the heater inlet water hose.
Disconnect the heater outlet water hose.
Remove the 3 nuts.
Disconnect the 2 ECM connectors and remove the ECM.

s3.ptxt: Raise the 2 levers while pushing the locks on the 2 levers, and disconnect the 2 ECM connectors.

atten3.ptxt: After disconnecting the connector, make sure that dirt, water and other foreign matter do not contact the connecting part of the connector.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 4 screws and 2 ECM brackets.
Disconnect the engine wire from the engine room junction block.

s3.ptxt: Remove the nut and separate the wire harness.
Using a screwdriver, unlock the engine room J/B. Pull the engine room J/B upward.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts and 2 clamps from the body.
Remove the bolt and clamp from the bracket.
Remove the bolt and disconnect the wire harness.
Disconnect the park/neutral position switch connector.
Remove the clip and nut, and separate the cable from the transaxle.
Remove the No. 1 fuel pipe clamp.
Disconnect the connector from the tube while pinching part A with your fingers as shown in the illustration.

item.ptxt: Check for contamination in the pipe and around the connector. Clean if necessary and then disconnect the connector.
Disconnect the connector by hand.
Do not bend, fold or rotate the nylon tube.
If the pipe and connector are stuck together, push and pull the connector until it becomes free.
Put the pipe and connector ends in vinyl bags to prevent damage and contamination.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT NO. 1 OIL RESERVOIR TO PUMP HOSE

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the No. 1 oil reservoir to pump hose.
Disconnect the return tube sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE EXHAUST PIPE NO. 1 SUPPORT BRACKET

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 nuts and front support brackets.
Disconnect the 2 heated oxygen sensor (bank 1, 2 sensor 2) connectors.
Remove the 2 nuts from the exhaust front pipe (left bank exhaust manifold side).
Remove the 2 nuts from the exhaust front pipe (right bank exhaust pipe manifold side).
Remove the exhaust front pipe.
Remove the 2 gaskets from the exhaust front pipe.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT AXLE HUB NUT LH

s2.ptxt: Using SST and a hammer, release the staked part of the front axle hub nut.

atten3.ptxt: Loosen the staked part of the nut completely, otherwise the thread of the drive shaft may be damaged.

s2.ptxt: While applying the brakes, remove the front axle hub nut.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT AXLE HUB NUT RH

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures described for the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT FRONT STABILIZER LINK ASSEMBLY LH

s2.ptxt: Remove the nut and separate the front stabilizer link assembly.

atten4.ptxt: If the ball joint turns together with the nut, use a hexagon wrench (6 mm) to hold the stud.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT FRONT STABILIZER LINK ASSEMBLY RH

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures described for the LH side.

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and clip, and separate the speed sensor wire and flexible hose from the shock absorber.
Remove the bolt and separate the speed sensor from the steering knuckle.

item.ptxt: Prevent foreign matter from adhering to the speed sensor.
Be careful not to damage the speed sensor.

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures described for the LH side.

s2.ptxt: Remove the cotter pin and nut.
Using SST, separate the tie rod end sub-assembly from the steering knuckle.

item.ptxt: Make sure that the string of the SST is securely tied to the vehicle.
Be careful not to damage the ball joint dust cover.
Be careful not to damage the steering knuckle.
Be careful not to damage the front disc brake dust cover.

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures described for the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT FRONT SUSPENSION LOWER NO. 1 ARM LH

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and 2 nuts, and separate the front suspension lower No. 1 arm from the lower ball joint.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT FRONT SUSPENSION LOWER NO. 1 ARM RH

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures described for the LH side.

s2.ptxt: Put matchmarks on the front drive shaft assembly and the axle hub.
Using a plastic hammer, separate the front drive shaft assembly from the front axle assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the drive shaft boot and speed sensor rotor.

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures described for the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 1 EXHAUST PIPE SUPPORT BRACKET

s2.ptxt: Remove the No. 1 exhaust pipe support bracket with the 2 bolts.
Remove the 2 bolts, exhaust pipe support bracket and flywheel housing under cover.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE DRIVE PLATE AND TORQUE CONVERTER CLUTCH SETTING BOLT

s2.ptxt: Turn the crankshaft to gain access and remove the 6 bolts while holding the crankshaft pulley bolt with a wrench.
Secure the steering wheel with the seat belt in order to prevent rotation.

atten4.ptxt: This operation is useful to prevent damage to the spiral cable.

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and slide the steering sliding yoke.

atten3.ptxt: Do not separate the steering sliding yoke from the power steering link assembly.

s2.ptxt: Put matchmarks on the steering sliding yoke and the power steering link assembly.
Separate the steering sliding yoke from the power steering link assembly.
Remove the terminal cap.
Remove the nut and disconnect the wire harness from terminal B.
Disconnect the generator connector from the generator assembly.
Disconnect the connector from the compressor and magnetic clutch.
Disconnect the 2 wire harness clamps.
Remove the 2 bolts.
Remove the bolt from the cylinder block.
Disconnect the wire harness clamp and remove the generator assembly.
Remove the bolt and wire harness clamp stay.
Remove the bolt and bracket.
Remove the 2 connector clamps.
Remove the 4 bolts and separate the compressor.

atten4.ptxt: Hang up the hoses instead of detaching them.

s2.ptxt: Set the engine lifter.
Remove the 4 bolts, 2 nuts, and frame side rail plates RH and LH.
Remove the 4 bolts, 2 nuts, and front suspension member brace rear RH and LH.
Operate the engine lifter, then remove the engine assembly from the vehicle.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the engine is clear of all wiring and hoses.

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the power steering oil pressure switch connector.
Remove the pressure feed tube clamp bolt.
Loosen the bolt A.
Remove the bolt B and vane pump.
Install the 2 engine hangers with the 4 bolts as shown in the illustration.
Attach the engine sling device and hang the engine with the chain block.
Disconnect the connector and clamp.
Disconnect the 2 clamps.
Remove the 2 nuts and disconnect the engine mounting insulators RH and LH.
Remove the bolt and disconnect the engine mounting insulator FR.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT DRIVE SHAFT ASSEMBLY LH

s2.ptxt: Using SST, remove the front drive shaft assembly LH.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the drive shaft dust cover, boot, and oil seal.
Be careful not to drop the drive shaft assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT DRIVE SHAFT ASSEMBLY RH

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, remove the bearing bracket hole snap ring.
Remove the bolt and front drive shaft assembly RH from the drive shaft bearing bracket.

atten3.ptxt: Do not damage the boot and oil seal.

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the terminal 50 connector from the starter assembly.
Remove the nut and disconnect the wire harness from terminal 30.
Remove the 2 bolts and starter assembly.
Remove the bolt and breather bracket from the camshaft housing sub-assembly LH.
Remove the breather hose from the breather bracket.
Remove the 11 bolts.
Separate and remove the automatic transaxle.
Put matchmarks on the transaxle housing and torque converter clutch assembly.
Remove the torque converter clutch assembly from the automatic transaxle assembly.
Remove the 3 bolts and engine mounting front bracket.
Remove the 2 bolts and TCM from the transaxle.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE SPEEDOMETER DRIVEN HOLE COVER SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and hole cover from the transaxle case.
Remove the O-ring from the hole cover.
Remove the 4 bolts and 4 clamp brackets.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 1 TRANSMISSION CONTROL CABLE BRACKET

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts and No.1 transmission control cable bracket.
Separate the oil cooler outlet hose.
Separate the oil cooler inlet hose.
